heading: Termination of private foundation status
label: 507 Termination of private foundation status
notes: Amendments1984—Subsec. (d)(2)(C). added subpar. (C).Pub. L. 98–3691976— struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 94–455

children.text: such organization notifies the Secretary (at such time and in such manner as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe) of its intent to accomplish such termination, or

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to such organization, there have been either willful repeated acts (or failures to act), or a willful and flagrant act (or failure to act), giving rise to liability for tax under chapter 42, and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary notifies such organization that, by reason of subparagraph (A), such organization is liable for the tax imposed by subsection (c),

children.heading: Transfer to, or operation as, public charity
children.label: 1 Transfer to, or operation as, public charity
A
children.text: such organization distributes all of its net assets to one or more organizations described in section 170(b)(1)(A) (other than in clauses (vii) and (viii)) each of which has been in existence and so described for a continuous period of at least 60 calendar months immediately preceding such distribution, or

children.label: i
children.text: such organization meets the requirements of paragraph (1), (2), or (3) of section 509(a) by the end of the 12-month period beginning with its first taxable year which begins after , or for a continuous period of 60 calendar months beginning with the first day of any taxable year which begins after ,December 31, 1969December 31, 1969

children.label: ii
children.text: such organization notifies the Secretary (in such manner as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe) before the commencement of such 12-month or 60-month period (or before the 90th day after the day on which regulations first prescribed under this subsection become final) that it is terminating its private foundation status, and

children.label: iii
children.text: such organization establishes to the satisfaction of the Secretary (in such manner as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe) immediately after the expiration of such 12-month or 60-month period that such organization has complied with clause (i).

children.heading: Transferee foundations
children.label: 2 Transferee foundations
children.text: For purposes of this part, in the case of a transfer of assets of any private foundation to another private foundation pursuant to any liquidation, merger, redemption, recapitalization, or other adjustment, organization, or reorganization, the transferee foundation shall not be treated as a newly created organization.

the amount which the private foundation substantiates by adequate records or other corroborating evidence as the aggregate tax benefit resulting from the section 501(c)(3) status of such foundation, or
the value of the net assets of such foundation.

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate increases in tax under chapters 1, 11, and 12 (or the corresponding provisions of prior law) which would have been imposed with respect to all substantial contributors to the foundation if deductions for all contributions made by such contributors to the foundation after , had been disallowed, andFebruary 28, 1913

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate increases in tax under chapter 1 (or the corresponding provisions of prior law) which would have been imposed with respect to the income of the private foundation for taxable years beginning after , if (i) it had not been exempt from tax under section 501(a) (or the corresponding provisions of prior law), and (ii) in the case of a trust, deductions under section 642(c) (or the corresponding provisions of prior law) had been limited to 20 percent of the taxable income of the trust (computed without the benefit of section 642(c) but with the benefit of section 170(b)(1)(A)), andDecember 31, 1912

children.label: C
children.text: interest on the increases in tax determined under subparagraphs (A) and (B) from the first date on which each such increase would have been due and payable to the date on which the organization ceases to be a private foundation.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: A Definition
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the term “substantial contributor” means any person who contributed or bequeathed an aggregate amount of more than $5,000 to the private foundation, if such amount is more than 2 percent of the total contributions and bequests received by the foundation before the close of the taxable year of the foundation in which the contribution or bequest is received by the foundation from such person. In the case of a trust, the term “substantial contributor” also means the creator of the trust.

children.label: i
children.text: each contribution or bequest shall be valued at fair market value on the date it was received,

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a foundation which is in existence on , all contributions and bequests received on or before such date shall be treated (except for purposes of clause (i)) as if received on such date,October 9, 1969

children.label: iii
children.text: an individual shall be treated as making all contributions and bequests made by his spouse, and

children.label: iv
children.text: any person who is a substantial contributor on any date shall remain a substantial contributor for all subsequent periods.

children.heading: Person ceases to be substantial contributor in certain cases
children.label: C Person ceases to be substantial contributor in certain cases
I
children.text: during the 10-year period ending at the close of such taxable year such person (and all related persons) have not made any contribution to such private foundation,

children.label: II
children.text: at no time during such 10-year period was such person (or any related person) a foundation manager of such private foundation, and

children.label: III
children.text: the aggregate contributions made by such person (and related persons) are determined by the Secretary to be insignificant when compared to the aggregate amount of contributions to such foundation by one other person.

children.heading: Related person
children.label: ii Related person
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the term “related person” means, with respect to any person, any other person who would be a disqualified person (within the meaning of section 4946) by reason of his relationship to such person. In the case of a contributor which is a corporation, the term also includes any officer or director of such corporation.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 3 Regulations
children.text: For purposes of this section, the determination as to whether and to what extent there would have been any increase in tax shall be made in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Value of assets
children.label: e Value of assets
children.text: For purposes of subsection (c), the value of the net assets shall be determined at whichever time such value is higher: (1) the first day on which action is taken by the organization which culminates in its ceasing to be a private foundation, or (2) the date on which it ceases to be a private foundation.

children.heading: Liability in case of transfers of assets from private foundation
children.label: f Liability in case of transfers of assets from private foundation
children.text: For purposes of determining liability for the tax imposed by subsection (c) in the case of assets transferred by the private foundation, such tax shall be deemed to have been imposed on the first day on which action is taken by the organization which culminates in its ceasing to be a private foundation.

the private foundation distributes all of its net assets to one or more organizations described in section 170(b)(1)(A) (other than in clauses (vii) and (viii)) each of which has been in existence and so described for a continuous period of at least 60 calendar months, or

following the notification prescribed in section 6104(c) to the appropriate State officer, such State officer within one year notifies the Secretary, in such manner as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe, that corrective action has been initiated pursuant to State law to insure that the assets of such private foundation are preserved for such charitable or other purposes specified in section 501(c)(3) as may be ordered or approved by a court of competent jurisdiction, and upon completion of the corrective action, the Secretary receives certification from the appropriate State officer that such action has resulted in such preservation of assets.
